description_long: This is an elongated, irregularly shaped stone with a reddish-brown coloration and subtle white patches scattered across its surface. The stone has a rough, natural texture with visible stratification and some chipped or broken edges, suggesting it was quarried or fractured from a larger rock. Its surface is mostly matte, with occasional areas of lighter, almost chalky deposits. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings, and its form is reminiscent of a sandstone block or slab. The overall shape is asymmetrical, with one side broader and flatter, while the opposite side tapers and narrows. This type of stone is commonly used as a barrier or obstacle in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped public spaces.
description: A rough, reddish-brown elongated stone with white patches and a natural, stratified texture. Its irregular shape and solid appearance make it suitable as an urban barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and flat, with visible stratification and a mix of reddish-brown and white patches.
description_view_1: A narrow, tapered end of the stone is visible, highlighting its thickness and irregular, jagged edge.
description_view_2: The opposite broad side is shown, with a smoother surface and more pronounced white markings.
description_view_3: The stone is seen from the other narrow end, revealing a slightly angled, uneven profile with layered textures.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle natural layering and stratification with irregular white patches and minor surface mottling.
attribute_weathering: Rough and slightly chipped, with some surface discoloration indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; may be partially surrounded by grass or mulch.
